question_latex: Which component of a computer system focuses on attributes that have a direct impact on the logical execution of a program?
solution_latex: Computer Architecture refers to those attributes of a system that have a direct impact on the logical execution of a program[cite: 1, 55].

question_latex: How is Computer Organization defined in relation to architecture?
option_a: It defines the instruction set of the system
option_b: It describes the high-level logical design
option_c: It refers to the operational units and their interconnections that realize architectural specifications
option_d: It focuses solely on the programming language used
solution_latex: Computer Organization refers to the operational units and their interconnections that realize the architectural specifications[cite: 1, 57].

question_latex: If a system has a 'multiply instruction', classifying this feature is an example of:
solution_latex: The existence of a multiply instruction is a computer architecture issue, while how that multiply is implemented is a computer organization issue[cite: 1, 58].

question_latex: Which of the following is NOT one of the three main categories of computer architecture?
solution_latex: The three main categories are System Design, Instruction Set Architecture, and Microarchitecture[cite: 115, 414].

question_latex: What does 'System Design' primarily encompass in computer architecture?
option_a: The instruction set used by programmers
option_b: The physical hardware parts like multiprocessors, memory controllers, and CPU
option_c: The translation of code into machine language
option_d: The specific algorithm for data storage
solution_latex: System design covers the hardware parts, including multiprocessors, memory controllers, CPU, data processors, and direct memory access[cite: 116].

question_latex: Which of the following is a core focus of Instruction Set Architecture (ISA)?
option_b: The CPU's capabilities, functions, and data formats
option_c: The type of peripheral cables used
option_d: The specific manufacturing technology of the memory
solution_latex: Instruction Set Architecture revolves around the CPU and includes its capabilities, functions, data formats, and register types[cite: 117, 118].

question_latex: What is the primary function of microarchitecture?
option_a: It defines the high-level system software
option_b: It determines the overall software compatibility
option_c: It defines the storage elements and data paths and how they are implemented
option_d: It determines the external interface of the computer
solution_latex: Microarchitecture defines the storage elements/data paths and how they will be implemented into the instruction set architecture[cite: 119].

question_latex: During the system design process, which is developed first?
solution_latex: Architecture is developed before computer organization during the system design process[cite: 414, 594].

question_latex: Computer architecture acts as a blueprint for:
solution_latex: Architecture describes what a computer does and serves as the blueprint for designing computer systems[cite: 413, 593].

question_latex: What is the main goal of deciding on instruction sets, data paths, and control units?
solution_latex: Decisions about the instruction set, data paths, and control units are made to optimize performance and functionality[cite: 412, 592].

question_latex: Which of these is transparent to the programmer?
solution_latex: Control signals are examples of organizational details that are transparent to the programmer[cite: 1, 57].

question_latex: If a CPU has a hardware multiply unit versus performing multiplication by repeated addition, this is a matter of:
solution_latex: How a feature (like a multiply instruction) is implemented is a computer organization issue[cite: 1, 58].

question_latex: Which attribute is considered part of computer architecture?
option_c: Number of bits used to represent data types
solution_latex: The number of bits used to represent various data types is an architectural issue[cite: 1, 56].

question_latex: What is the role of the CPU in the system design category?
option_a: It acts as a storage medium only
option_b: It is the part which makes a program run
option_c: It is exclusively for peripheral control
option_d: It only handles the data bus
solution_latex: The CPU is the part in a computer which makes a program run, whether it is the operating system or an application[cite: 119].

question_latex: What do the operational units of a computer realize?
solution_latex: Computer Organization refers to the operational units and their interconnections that realize the architectural specifications[cite: 1, 57].

question_latex: How do hardware components like CPU and memory interact in a computer system?
option_a: They work together to execute instructions efficiently
option_b: They act independently of the instruction set
option_c: They focus exclusively on user input
option_d: They determine the language of the software
solution_latex: Computer architecture focuses on how a computer's hardware components, like the CPU and memory, work together to execute instructions efficiently[cite: 411, 591].

question_latex: Which of these is NOT an architectural feature visible to a programmer?
solution_latex: Memory technology is an organizational/microarchitectural detail, not typically visible to the programmer[cite: 1, 57].

question_latex: What is the definition of computer architecture in terms of functional design?
option_a: It is the set of peripheral connections
option_b: It is the functional design and structure of a computer system
option_c: It is the physical manufacturing process
option_d: It is the method of debugging software
solution_latex: Computer architecture is the functional design and structure of a computer system[cite: 410, 590].

question_latex: Which components are included in the System Design category?
solution_latex: System design includes hardware parts like multiprocessors, memory controllers, CPU, data processors, and direct memory access[cite: 116].

question_latex: Why are Intel x86 family computers considered to have the same architecture?
option_a: They have the exact same hardware organization
option_b: They share the same basic architecture and provide code compatibility
option_c: They use the same memory technology
option_d: They use the same physical casing
solution_latex: All Intel x86 family members share the same basic architecture, which allows for code compatibility[cite: 59].

question_latex: In computer architecture, what determines the maximum memory capacity of a system?
solution_latex: The width of the address bus determines the maximum memory capacity of the system[cite: 14].

question_latex: What is the primary role of the Control Bus?
option_c: Providing timing information and control signals like memory read/write
solution_latex: The control bus carries control and timing information, such as memory read/write and interrupt requests[cite: 14].

question_latex: What issue can arise when there are too many devices on a bus?
solution_latex: A great number of devices on a bus causes propagation delay (time to coordinate use) and potentially makes the bus a bottleneck[cite: 14].

question_latex: What is the function of an Instruction Set?
option_b: Representing the set of commands that the CPU can execute
option_c: Managing the cooling system of the CPU
solution_latex: The instruction set is a fundamental part of architecture, representing the commands available to the programmer[cite: 1, 5, 59].

question_latex: What is another name for Computer Organization?
solution_latex: Computer Organization is frequently called microarchitecture[cite: 54].

question_latex: If you are designing a computer, which component is typically fixed first?
solution_latex: For designing a computer, its architecture (Instruction Set Architecture) is fixed first[cite: 53].

question_latex: Computer Architecture deals with functional behavior, while Computer Organization deals with:
solution_latex: Computer architecture deals with functional behavior, while Computer Organization deals with structural relationships[cite: 53].

question_latex: Which of the following is NOT a hardware part associated with System Design?
solution_latex: Addressing modes are part of Instruction Set Architecture, not the hardware parts defined in System Design[cite: 116].

question_latex: What is the primary difference between a high-level design issue and a low-level design issue?
option_a: High-level relates to organization, low-level to architecture
option_b: High-level relates to architecture, low-level to organization
solution_latex: Architecture generally deals with high-level design issues, while organization deals with low-level design issues[cite: 53].

question_latex: Why is 'Architecture' visible to the programmer?
option_a: Because it relates to how the CPU is manufactured
option_b: Because it defines the interface (e.g., instruction set) the programmer uses
option_c: Because it defines the internal power supply units
option_d: Because it focuses on peripheral connections only
solution_latex: Architecture is visible to the programmer because it includes elements like the instruction set and registers that the programmer uses to write code[cite: 5].

question_latex: What role does the Data Bus play?
option_a: It identifies the source of data
solution_latex: The data bus carries data, and there is no difference between “data” and “instruction” at this level[cite: 14].

question_latex: What is the impact of bus width on a system?
option_a: It has no impact on performance
option_b: It is a key determinant of performance
solution_latex: The width of the data bus is a key determinant of performance[cite: 14].

question_latex: Which components are physically realized in Computer Organization?
solution_latex: Computer Organization consists of physical units like circuit designs, peripherals, and adders[cite: 53].

question_latex: What does a cache structure do in bus architecture?
option_b: It insulates the CPU from frequent accesses to main memory
option_c: It increases the number of buses needed
solution_latex: Traditional hierarchical bus architecture uses a cache structure to insulate the CPU from frequent accesses to main memory[cite: 14].

question_latex: Which of these is NOT managed by the instruction set architecture?
solution_latex: Physical memory module manufacturer is an organizational detail, not part of the instruction set architecture[cite: 118].

question_latex: What allows for backward code compatibility in a family of computer systems?
option_d: Manufacturing computers in the same factory
solution_latex: Sharing the same basic architecture gives code compatibility, at least backwards[cite: 59].

question_latex: What does an address bus do?
option_b: Identifies the source or destination of data
solution_latex: The address bus identifies the source or destination of data, such as a location in memory[cite: 14].

question_latex: Computer Organization is often referred to by which term?
solution_latex: Computer Organization is frequently called microarchitecture[cite: 53].

question_latex: When designing a computer, what is decided after its architecture?
solution_latex: For designing a computer, an organization is decided after its architecture[cite: 53].

question_latex: Why is the instruction set considered an architectural attribute?
option_a: It defines the performance of the memory
option_b: It is directly visible to the programmer and affects code execution
option_c: It is transparent to the programmer
option_d: It defines the physical connections of the CPU
solution_latex: The instruction set is visible to the programmer and is a key architectural attribute[cite: 1, 5, 59].

question_latex: Which is a valid example of a Control Signal?
solution_latex: Memory read/write signals are control signals, which are an aspect of computer organization[cite: 14].

question_latex: What is the primary function of a Memory Controller in System Design?
option_b: Managing access to and from memory
solution_latex: Memory controllers are hardware parts included in system design that manage memory access[cite: 116].

question_latex: If a computer is a Von Neumann system, which architectural approach does it follow?
solution_latex: Von Neumann systems can be implemented using a single bus architecture[cite: 53].

question_latex: What is meant by the 'Instruction Set Architecture' revolving around the CPU?
option_a: It means the CPU is the only component in the architecture
option_b: It means it focuses on the CPU's capabilities, registers, and instructions
option_c: It means the CPU is the most expensive part of the computer
option_d: It means the CPU cannot interact with memory
solution_latex: ISA revolves around the CPU because it defines the functions, capabilities, registers, and instructions that the CPU provides to the programmer[cite: 117, 118].

question_latex: Which of these defines how instructions are executed efficiently?
solution_latex: Architecture focuses on how a computer's components work together to execute instructions efficiently[cite: 411, 591].

question_latex: What is the benefit of defining a standardized architecture for a family of computers?
option_a: It makes the computers look better
option_b: It allows for backward code compatibility
option_c: It eliminates the need for any organization
solution_latex: Standardized architecture gives code compatibility, allowing programs written for older models to run on newer ones[cite: 59].

question_latex: What does microarchitecture do to data paths?
option_b: It defines the storage elements and data paths
option_d: It makes them visible to the programmer
solution_latex: Microarchitecture defines the storage elements and data paths of a system[cite: 119].

question_latex: What is the function of the 'Bus Request' control signal?
option_b: To request control of the bus
solution_latex: Bus request is a standard control signal used when a device wants to gain control of the bus[cite: 14].

question_latex: What is true about the distinction between data and instructions at the bus level?
option_a: They are kept on separate buses
option_b: There is no difference between them
option_c: Instructions have their own dedicated bus
option_d: Data is only sent to memory
solution_latex: There is no difference between “data” and “instruction” at the data bus level[cite: 14].

question_latex: Which system component is directly involved in Direct Memory Access (DMA)?
solution_latex: Direct Memory Access (DMA) is a hardware component/capability listed under System Design[cite: 116].

question_latex: Why is architecture referred to as Instruction Set Architecture (ISA)?
option_b: Because it comprises logical functions like instruction sets, registers, and addressing modes
option_c: Because it deals only with compilers
option_d: Because it only describes the CPU's exterior
solution_latex: Computer Architecture is also called ISA because it comprises logical functions such as instruction sets, registers, data types, and addressing modes[cite: 53].

question_latex: Which component focuses on the physical units like adders?
solution_latex: Computer Organization consists of physical units like circuit designs, peripherals, and adders[cite: 53].

question_latex: What does a reset control signal do?
solution_latex: Reset is a standard control signal used to reset the system state[cite: 14].

question_latex: What is the relation between architecture and design?
option_b: Architecture serves as the blueprint for designing systems
option_d: Design is only concerned with software
solution_latex: Architecture serves as the blueprint for designing computer systems[cite: 593].

question_latex: Which type of design issue is 'Memory Technology'?
solution_latex: Memory technology relates to the physical implementation, which is a low-level design issue under Computer Organization[cite: 53].

question_latex: Which factor most limits aggregate data transfer in traditional bus architecture?
option_b: Demand approaching the capacity of the bus
solution_latex: The bus becomes a bottleneck as the aggregate data transfer demand approaches the capacity of the bus[cite: 14].

question_latex: What is the role of an interface between the computer and its peripherals?
option_b: It is an architectural issue only
option_c: It is not a design issue
option_d: It is purely a software issue
solution_latex: Interfaces between computer and peripherals are organizational issues[cite: 57].

question_latex: Which design issue focuses on the hardware implementation of instructions?
solution_latex: Microarchitecture deals with the physical implementation of the ISA, such as data paths and control signal generation[cite: 119].

question_latex: Why are multiprocessors categorized under System Design?
option_b: Because they are hardware units that define the system structure
option_d: Because they only function as registers
solution_latex: System design includes hardware parts, specifically multiprocessors, as they form part of the actual computer system structure[cite: 116].
